column1: Data Supplied By: National Livestock Reporting Service
Data source: National Livestock Reporting Service
Please see terms of use here: https://www.mla.com.au/general/Terms-and-conditions/data-and-information/

With the rain yet to hit the drawing area numbers were up by 200 head as Singleton agents penned 1097 cattle. The magical $6 mark was broken through for steer calves as the $5 mark was broken through for heifer calves on a day where commission buyers had fistfuls of new orders following the rain event statewide.

Under 200kg steer calves up to 100c/kg dearer 570c to 612c/kg, next level up to 62c/kg better off making from 472c to 598c/kg averaging 567c/kg. Light heifer calves sold 50c/kg dearer 434c to 544c/kg with local trade calves making from 450c to 538c/kg. 

The few medium and heavy weight feeder yearling steers sold in a range of 388c to 502c/kg, same article in the yearling heifers made from 332c to 466c/kg with the over 400kg heavy weights averaging around 455c/kg.

Heavy steers and Bullocks to process made from 398c to 480c/kg. Heavy grown heifers 378c to 420c/kg. Despite a southern processor not operating the 150 cows rallied with light 2 scores selling between 240c and 340c/kg, heavy 3 and 4 scores improved 26c/kg trading between 350c and 411c/kg.

Market Operator Angus Barlow.
